summary:
Only include essential facts directly related to the composition of the square knot in surgical context—no tangents about other types of knots or non-surgical applications.

The square knot, also known as a surgeon’s knot, is constructed using a double overhand throw followed by a single overhand throw in the opposite direction, which functionally comprises two single loops thrown in opposing directions. This configuration provides secure anchoring during surgical suturing and aligns with standardized medical definitions found across reliable surgical resources.
